name: mild_day_planned_window_no_pause
description: Mild day with windows_recommended=True and planned window 10:00–17:00. Outdoor is cooler than indoor. Sensor opens at 12:00 (inside the window). The planned-window gate fires — production returns silently with no new event. The last observable decision at 12:00 remains classification_applied.
notes: The planned-window gate at position 3 fires BEFORE nat-vent evaluation when within window.
When the gate fires, production simply returns — no event is emitted. The last visible decision
stays at classification_applied. This is correct: planned window tells CA to stay out of the way.
outdoor=65F < indoor=73F, indoor=73F > comfort_heat=70F, outdoor < threshold — nat-vent conditions met,
but gate takes priority and the return happens before any event emission.
Covers Priority 6 mild_day_planned_window from plan abundant-mapping-clarke.md (C5×E1 cell).

events.note: Late morning: indoor 73F, outdoor 65F — nat-vent conditions would be favorable
Window opens at 12:00 inside the 10:00–17:00 planned window — gate fires, production returns silently

assertions.reason: Mild-day classification applied: hvac_mode=off, windows_recommended=True, 10:00–17:00 window

sensor_open during planned window period — production _is_within_planned_window_period() returns True and handle_door_window_open() returns silently with no event emitted. No pause, no nat-vent event. Last observable decision remains classification_applied. CA stays hands-off during the occupant's planned window period.

verdict.summary: Planned window gate suppresses sensor_open response on mild day — production returns silently, no event emitted
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (off, windows recommended) → sensor_open at 12:00 → no new event; last decision stays classification_applied
verdict.expected_behavior: Sensor open inside planned window suppressed entirely; CA stays hands-off during window period
